title: ClinicalProfile
description: Structured patient data extracted from a free-text clinical referral. Produced by the IntakeAgent and consumed by the LiteratureAgent and ConfigAgent downstream. Every field is optional except the ones marked required — missing fields trigger literature-backed defaults in later stages, not hard failures.

patient_id.description: Anonymised identifier used in case directories (e.g. 'BPM120'). Optional — if the referral does not state one the coordinator will assign a placeholder.

age_years.description: Patient age in whole years. Null if not stated.

sex.description: Biological sex for Reynolds scaling (cardiac output varies). Use 'unknown' rather than guessing.

bsa_m2.description: Body surface area in square metres (DuBois or Mosteller). Used for flow normalisation.

diagnosis.description: Primary cardiovascular diagnosis, in the referrer's own words where possible. Required — this drives downstream parameter selection.

diagnosis_severity_hint.description: A qualitative or quantitative severity marker explicitly mentioned in the referral (e.g. 'peak echo gradient 35 mmHg', 'dilated to 45 mm', 'post-operative day 14'). Do not invent.

heart_rate_bpm.description: Resting heart rate in bpm. Sets the cardiac cycle period (T = 60/HR).

systolic_bp_mmhg.description: Systolic blood pressure from the arm cuff, in mmHg.

diastolic_bp_mmhg.description: Diastolic blood pressure from the arm cuff, in mmHg.

cardiac_output_l_min.description: Estimated cardiac output in L/min (from echo, MRI, or thermodilution). If missing, the config stage will use a BSA-normalised default.

imaging_modality.description: Imaging modalities mentioned in the referral. Drives inlet BC choice (MRI ⇒ boundaryData mapping, others ⇒ analytical profile).

flow_waveform_source.description: Where the inlet flow waveform will come from. The agent fills this based on what the referral mentions; ConfigAgent later resolves the actual file path.

study_goal.description: One-sentence summary of what the CFD study is meant to assess (e.g. 'pressure drop and WSS around the coarctation'). Used to pick the mesh goal preset.

constraints.description: Hard constraints stated by the referrer — runtime budget, required physics model, must-use Windkessel settings, etc. Each entry is a short clause. The ConfigAgent treats these as non-negotiable.

missing_fields.description: List of standard fields (heart_rate_bpm, systolic_bp_mmhg, etc.) that the referral did NOT state. The downstream rationale uses this to explain which values came from defaults.

confidence.description: IntakeAgent's self-reported confidence. 'high' when the referral is unambiguous and complete, 'low' when key fields were guessed from context.

notes.description: Free-text caveats the agent wants to flag to the clinician (e.g. 'HR value inferred from cycle period'). Optional.
